feed.title: SAP Community - BW SAP HANA Data Warehousing
feed.subtitle: BW SAP HANA Data Warehousing Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: max. excel export in BW4web
entry.content:
Hello,

we're on BW4HANA SP2.

How can I increase the max. number of excel rows in the export function of the transaction BW4WEB ?

I found no possibilities in the settings.

 

Thanks

entry.title: BW Transformation end routine populated field list
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

despite what I can see in structure, in end routine the result_package type _ty_s_TG_1 contains all fields, including the ones not mapped with any rule.

Investigating in generated program code I found the method get_fields_populated0001, but neither this method nor the output table are visible in end routine.

How can I get this field list?

Thanks in advance

Dario

entry.title: Unable to write select query in BW transformations
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to display current date in one of the field routine of the BW transformations. I wrote the below code in field routine and getting the error message as
Incorrect syntax error near :CUR_DATE.

outtab = select (select current_date into CUR_DATE from dummy AS "CUR_DATE"),
"RECORD",
"SQL__PROCEDURE__SOURCE__RECORD"
from :INTAB;

Please guide me how to solve this error.

Thanks.

entry.title: How to call Class inside Transformation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to call my class in one of my BW transformations.

To make it simple, I just created a Class (SYSTEM_DATE_CLASS) which will give the output as System date.

In my BW transformation, I just created a new Info-object "CUR_DATE" in my ADSO. While loading the date to ADSO, I need to write a field routine by calling the system date Class and populate the date for the field CUR_DATE in the ADSO. I dont know, how to call class inside transformation with variable. I am getting error in Class implementation in Outtab.

Invalid character string "(" in "ZDELETE=>AMDP_SYSTEM_DATE(". Only transparent database tables, views, or database procedures can be specified after USING.

 

CLASS SYSTEM_DATE_CLASS DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .
  PUBLIC SECTION.
*AMDP Class 
  INTERFACES if_amdp_marker_hdb.
*declare Class method amdp_system_date
  class-methods amdp_system_date
  exporting
  value(i_sydate) type D.
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS SYSTEM_DATE_CLASS IMPLEMENTATION.
METHOD amdp_system_date by database procedure
                   for hdb
                   language sqlscript
                   options read-only.

i_sydate = session_context( 'SAP_SYSTEM_DATE' );
ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

BW transformation Code :

 

class ZCLKMCRK3HNI3A57NCPAQHC_M definition
  public
  create public .

public section.
 interfaces IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB .
   types:

methods S0001_G01_R18
    importing
      value(i_req_dtp_name) type STRING
      value(i_req_logsys) type STRING
      value(i_req_src_name) type STRING
      value(i_req_src_type) type STRING
      value(i_req_tgt_name) type STRING
      value(i_req_tgt_type) type STRING
      value(i_req_requid) type STRING
      value(i_sydate) type D
      value(inTab) type TN_T_IN_S0001_G01_R18
    exporting
      value(outTab) type TN_T_OUT_S0001_G01_R18 .
protected section.
private section.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS ZCLKMCRK3HNI3A57NCPAQHC_M IMPLEMENTATION.
METHOD S0001_G01_R18 BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY USING zdelete=>amdp_system_date( i_sydate ).

outtab = select ( CALL "ZDELETE=>AMDP_SYSTEM_DATE( I_SYDATE )" ) AS "CUR_DATE",
                 "RECORD",
                 "SQL__PROCEDURE__SOURCE__RECORD"
                 from :INTAB;

errortab = SELECT NULL AS "ERROR_TEXT",
                  NULL AS "SQL__PROCEDURE__SOURCE__RECORD"
                  FROM "DUMMY";

ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: SAP HANA: Correct source System for Packages in HANA DEV and PROD
entry.content:
Hi experts,
I'm quite new in HANA DB and I need your help regarding the source system of packages on HANA DB in our PROD and DEV system.
We have two BW 7.5 on HANA systems.
BW PROD -> PB1
BW DEV-> TB2
I would like to correct the "SRC_SYSTEM" in "_SYS_REPO"."PACKAGE_CATALOG" on our PROD and DEV BW HANA.
In screenshot "HANA DEV (TB2) content" you can see the packages in HANA Studio in DEV system.
In screenshot "HANA PROD (PB1) content" you can see the packages in HANA Studio in DEV system.
I also made an Excel File as comparison between the two systems where the source system is NOT "SAP".
Here my SQL Statement for reference:

SELECT * FROM "_SYS_REPO"."PACKAGE_CATALOG" where SRC_SYSTEM!='SAP'

Please let me know if I have forgotten any important information for you.
I'm new to this area.

Best regards,
Martin

entry.title: SAP HANA
How to display landed cost of multiped PO
entry.content: How I can get the landed cost list against PO list. If I am not authorized for the SAP Tables. I want to know the standard T. Code for that if SAP provided.

entry.title: S4 Brownfield (Native HANA)
entry.content:
Hi,

Requesting your inputs here. Below is my question pls. 

#1

In S/4 Brownfield Migration, will the tables be put in different schema? the source system has Enterprise HANA (Native HANA), do we need to replace all the tables within HANA Calculation views as ECC.MARA --> S4.MARA

# 2  

Or, in S/4 brownfields migration, the changes happen in the same schema? Is there any need to replace tables within HANA calculation views or SAP S/4HANA, will adjust these Calculation Views to account for the new table structures and data models. Appreciate your reply on this. Thanks

entry.title: Exporting BEx Queries from BW on HANA to BW4HANA??
entry.content:
Has anybody recently been involved with migration from BW on HANA to BW4HANA?

I want to know how you met the challenge of migrating BEx queries to BW4HANA.

Are there any new tools available? If not what is the least complicated way to migrate.

If there is no SAP tool is anybody aware of any third-party tool.

I need this information urgently, and any information will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Broadcasting SAP BW Analysis for Office report
entry.content: I am looking to broadcast SAP BW Analysis for Office report, Is it possible? If yes, please guide.

entry.title: s4hana po error
entry.content:
hi 

pls advise how to fix below error 

po shows shipped 8pcs 

but actually GR qty is 2pcs

i tried to GR balance 6pcs but PO has no open qty .

need to close short 6pcs but unable to amend PO# qty .

entry.title: SAP HANA Workspace
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I need information workspaces in HANA studio. In Repository what if we select Default value when creating a workspace, will it impact the existing objects import. What is the way to revert the workspace to default without having any impact on existing objects and schemas. 

Regards,

BD

entry.title: Missing lines in HANA Star join
entry.content:
Good morning experts,

I am currently facing a strange behavior and I am a bit dry on where to search. Any idea would be appreciated.

I created a complex Star join CV, calling various dimensions. This CV is built in 2 steps. The first one is to join the proper underlying tables to create the fact table. Then, in the star join, this fact table is used as the pivot for dimensions. So far, I don't expect any issue (data wise).

All relations in the star schema are left-outer joins on different parts of the fact table. 

When I run a preview of the fact table (so the point of entry of the star schema) I retrieve the data I need! First step is OK.

But, then, when I run a data preview at the start schema level, I am missing some lines, even if the joins between the fact table and the dimensions are left-outer....I also tried s direct SQL on the CV filtering on the specific data I am missing. 

Does one of you have an idea where the issue could be coming from ?

PY

entry.title: The Process Chain detail screen is not displaying the complete count of runs and error
entry.content:
The detail screen for any Process Chain is not showing the full count of runs and error counts.

Environment: SAP_BASIS=756 ; DWCORE=300

Thanks in advance for any help and support.

Thanks,

Vishala.

entry.title: BW Query create cumulative value
entry.content:
Hello All,

Kindly please check below 2 scenario's and help to create the BW Query in BW4HANA.

Scenario 1:

We have the values like below

GL Account Costcenter fiscal year period value1 value2 value3 value4 etc.

  123456      2345          001.2024             100     200     300       400

                                       002.2024

                                        003.2024 etc

 

if we select the fiscalyear period 001.2024 value1 should be show =100

if we select the fiscalyear period 001.2024 to 002. 2024 value should be shown like 300 (Value 100 +200)

if we select the fiscalyear period 001,2024 to 003.2024 value should be shown like  600 (Value 100+200+300) like 12 periods.

Scenario 2:

if we select the period 001.2024 value should be 100

if we select the period 002.2004 value should be 200

if we select the period 003.2024 value should be 300 show like 12 periods

Please check and help me to create the new BW Query.

Thanks and Regards,

Venkat.

entry.title: Loading BW Data into ACDOCU (SAP HANA)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm looking for the most efficient way to load data from our BW  into the ACDOCU table in SAP HANA. Could anyone shed some light on the following:

Standard vs. Custom: Is there a standard SAP-provided mechanism for this kind of data transfer, or do we need to involve our ABAP team for a custom solution?

Any insights, tips, or code snippets would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: CDS Entity View as data source in SAP Data Services Designer
entry.content:
I am experiencing an issue with using a CDS entity view as a data source in SAP Data Services Designer. When attempting to import it from SAP S/4HANA to BODS, the CDS entity view name is not visible, unlike classic CDS views.

Note: The CDS entity view has OData.publish active, and the service is also active.

Could someone explain why the CDS entity view is not appearing and how I might resolve this issue?

entry.title: RTE:[89134] Proxy server connect: Host unreachable
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am encountering  the following error when trying Connecting to SAP Datasphere from python via hdbcli

(5921, 'Unable to connect remote source: [SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB SO][HDBODBC] Communication link failure;-10709 Connection failed (RTE:[89134] Proxy server connect: Host unreachable)

this is what I am trying to do -
cursor = connection.cursor()
dbExampleView = "VW_ZZ_PCARD_HOLDERS"
sql_command = f'select top 10000 * from "{dbConsumptionSchema}"."{dbExampleView}"'
print(sql_command)
cursor.execute(sql_command)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
df = pd.Dataframe(rows)
display(df) 

How can I fix this error? and what could be causing this problem

entry.title: SAP BW 7.5 to SAP Datasphere: Why It’s Time to Skip SAP BW/4HANA and Embedded Analytics
entry.content:
Why Migrate from SAP BW 7.5 to SAP Datasphere?

1. End of Support:
SAP BW 7.5: Mainstream maintenance ends in 2027, with extended support until 2030.
Long-term Viability: After 2030, SAP BW will become obsolete, with no further innovations expected.

2. Innovation:
SAP Datasphere offers advanced features like AI integration, Data Fabric, Data Marketplace, and real-time analytics—none of which are available in SAP BW 7.5.

3. Skipping SAP BW/4HANA:
Limited Innovation: SAP BW/4HANA focuses on traditional data warehousing and lacks modern capabilities like AI, data democratization, or seamless cloud integration.
Similar Transition Effort: Migrating to SAP BW/4HANA requires significant effort and cost, similar to moving to SAP Datasphere. However, SAP Datasphere positions you directly in a future-proof, cloud-based environment.
Future Roadmap: SAP is heavily investing in SAP Datasphere as its flagship data solution, whereas BW/4HANA will not see major advancements in cutting-edge technologies.

4. Skipping SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics:
Scope Limitations: Embedded Analytics in SAP S/4HANA is designed for operational reporting within the S/4HANA environment. It lacks the breadth and depth needed for complex, enterprise-wide analytics.
Data Integration: Embedded Analytics focuses on real-time insights within S/4HANA but does not support extensive data integration across diverse sources like SAP Datasphere does.
-Scalability: For large-scale, cross-functional analytics and data management, SAP Datasphere offers a more scalable and comprehensive solution.

5. Future-Proofing:
- **Data Strategy Alignment**: With SAP Datasphere, you align with SAP’s broader strategy of integrating modern data concepts like Data Mesh and knowledge graphs, preparing your business for the future.

 Pros and Cons of Migrating to SAP Datasphere

Pros:
 Modern Capabilities: Access to the latest data management technologies.
Scalability: Built for the cloud, allowing seamless scaling.
Integration: Easier integration with other SAP cloud solutions like SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).
Flexibility: Supports diverse data use cases beyond traditional reporting.

Cons:
Migration Complexity: Moving from on-premise to cloud can be challenging.
Initial Cost: Investment in new infrastructure and training.
Learning Curve: Teams need to adapt to new tools and processes.

Steps for Migrating to SAP Datasphere

1. Assessment:
Inventory: Identify the data models, reports, and processes currently running in SAP BW 7.5.
Business Needs: Align migration with business goals and future data strategies.

2.Preparation
Data Cleansing: Clean up and optimize existing data to ensure smooth migration.
Training: Upskill your team on SAP Datasphere tools and features.

3.Proof of Concept (PoC)
Pilot Project: Start with a small, non-critical workload to test the migration process.

4. Data Migration:
Replication Flow: Use replication flow for real-time data transfer from SAP BW to SAP Datasphere.
Transformation: Map and transform data structures from SAP BW to match the new SAP Data sphere model.

5.Validation
Data Consistency: Ensure data integrity post-migration.
Performance Testing: Validate the performance of reports and analytics.

6.Go Live:
Cutover: Move production workloads to SAP Datasphere.
Monitor: Continuously monitor performance and resolve any post-migration issues.

7.Optimisation
Leverage New Features:Implement advanced capabilities such as AI/ML and Data Marketplace in your workflows.
Continuous Improvement: Regularly refine data models and processes.

When to Migrate?

Start Early: Ideally, begin planning by 2025 to allow sufficient time for a smooth transition before SAP BW 7.5 support ends.
Staggered Approach: Gradually move workloads, allowing time to adapt and optimize.

Conclusion

By skipping SAP BW/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics, and moving directly to SAP Datasphere, you ensure your data strategy is built on a modern, scalable, and future-proof foundation that aligns with the latest advancements in data management and analytics.

entry.title: SAP CDS view move from NONDESNOTCONNECTED to a new Catalog
entry.content:
Good morning,

I created a SAP CDS in S/4.  The CDS view is in BW under NONDESNOTCONNECTED. I want to move the CDS to its proper catalog. We're on BW on HANA.

Right mouse on the object does not provide an option to move the object.

Can you please describe the process for moving the CDS view into the correct catalog?

Thank you for your support.
